event.summary: Entered validation with a deterministic command, instruction, graph, and device-impact plan.

acceptance.proof: Contract, semantic-bridge, wire, and operational/ecosystem classes each have bounded cases and claim rules, with every current result remaining not implemented.
The black-box oracle process, fixture classifications, licenses, digests, negative cases, and source-input prohibitions are deterministic and public-safe.
The facade remains dependency-free, unpublished, source-only, and unable to create runtime or platform effects.

document.commit_policy: Commit the source-only compatibility baseline after standard validation; do not combine it with runtime implementation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Keep compatibility, authority, provenance, and validation routing aligned with the accepted baseline.
Expose only the compatibility level actually supported by evidence.
Route the new public Rusty LSL repository and preserve its authority boundary.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill diff and run its repository whitespace gate.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Confirm existing authority and compatibility-class guidance covers the baseline without introducing a repo-specific recipe.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and record whether a general rule changes.

non_scope: Protocol constants, wire behavior, discovery, clocks, inlets, outlets, recovery, networking, FFI, or native-library loading.
Cargo dependencies or production integration with official liblsl.
Copied or translated liblsl or rLSL implementation source.
Manifold authority, Morphospace-native transport, platform lifecycle, Quest or Hostess adapters, and device validation.
document.objective: Define a reproducible, independently authored LSL compatibility and provenance baseline before any protocol or runtime implementation begins.
outputs: Independently authored compatibility behavior catalog and damaged-case matrix.
Black-box official-liblsl oracle procedure and provenance manifest contract.
Instruction-impact review and compact iteration event.
